steps.canonical_text: Do not touch them while they are still in contact with electricity. Turn off the power at the main switch or unplug the source first.
If the source is a high-voltage line, fallen power line, or industrial machine, stay at least 6 meters away and call emergency services. Only utility workers can make it safe.
If the power cannot be turned off and the source is low voltage, you can use a dry wooden stick, broom handle, or similar non-conductive object to push them away. Never use anything wet or metal.
Once it is safe, check if they respond and whether they are breathing normally.
If they are not breathing or only gasping, call emergency services now and start chest compressions. Use an AED as soon as one is available.
Cool any burns at entry or exit points under cool running water and cover loosely with a clean cloth. Do not move them if a fall may have hurt their neck or back.
